source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure EFS and VPC Integration

input_row.source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure EFS and VPC Integration
input_row.description: You can use EFS as a network file system across availability zones on a virtual private cloud. This capability allows the organization to create a highly available file sharing solution. Leveraging AWS VPC and EC2 in tandem with AWS EFS makes for a highly available and scalable cloud file storage solution.
input_row.rationale: Redundancy and scalability are crucial for maintaining uninterrupted services. By integrating these AWS services, users can harness the full power of AWS, ensuring a resilient and scalable infrastructure. Impact: Not integrating AWS services for redundancy and scalability can lead to service disruptions and increased downtime. This approach also limits your ability to efficiently handle growing workloads, negatively impacting performance and user experience.
input_row.audit: Audit Procedures for AWS Redundancy and Scalability 1. Create Mount Targets in Each Availability Zone : Ensure EFS is attached in each availability zone by creating mount targets in each subnet. Although multiple subnets can exist per availability zone, verify that EFS is configured to work with one subnet per zone to maintain redundancy. 2. Monitor EFS with CloudWatch : Use AWS CloudWatch to automatically monitor your EFS service. Check that alarms are configured and logs and events are tracked effectively, providing real-time insights into the performance and health of your file systems.
input_row.remediation: Create an EC2 instance in each availability zone within your VPC. References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/efs/latest/ug/how-it-works.html#how-it-works- conceptual
input_row.impact: Not integrating AWS services for redundancy and scalability can lead to service disruptions and increased downtime. This approach also limits your ability to efficiently handle growing workloads, negatively impacting performance and user experience.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit requirements for ensuring EFS and VPC integration can be validated using AWS APIs. The key aspects of this control include verifying the creation of EFS mount targets in each availability zone and monitoring EFS with CloudWatch, both of which are accessible programmatically through AWS services.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_efs_mount_targets_in_azs_and_cloudwatch_monitoring_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Calls or boto3 SDK Methods to Use:**
   - Use `boto3.client('efs').describe_mount_targets()` to verify that EFS mount targets exist in each availability zone for the file system.
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()` to check for the presence of CloudWatch alarms specifically set up for monitoring the EFS file system.

2. **AWS CLI Commands (if applicable):**
   - `aws efs describe-mount-targets --file-system-id <file-system-id>` to list all mount targets and confirm distribution across AZs.
   - `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --alarm-name-prefix <prefix>` to confirm the existence of alarms for EFS.

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - Enumerate all EFS file systems across the AWS account to ensure each is compliant.
   - Check all related CloudWatch alarms for coverage and effectiveness in monitoring.

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Ensure each EFS file system has at least one mount target in every availability zone where its associated VPC has subnets.
   - Confirm CloudWatch alarms are configured to monitor critical metrics related to the EFS file system, such as burst credit balance, data read/write IO, etc.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - Pass: EFS has mount targets in all relevant AZs and corresponding CloudWatch alarms are configured.
   - Fail: Missing mount targets in any AZ or lack of CloudWatch monitoring and alarms for the EFS file system. 

This approach ensures comprehensive validation of EFS and VPC integration as well as the monitoring setup, aligning with the requirements specified in the CIS AWS Storage Services Benchmark.
